annotations.label: Axiom schema of replacement (informal)

anchor.snippet: the axiom schema of replacement is a schema of axioms in Zermelo–Fraenkel set theory

annotations.note: Mathlib encodes replacement via the `Definable₁` typeclass plus `ZFSet.image`, giving the image of any definable set function, but does not state the axiom schema as such.

annotations.label: Image of a set under a definable function

anchor.snippet: is called the image of

annotations.note: `ZFSet.image f x` (Mathlib/SetTheory/ZFC/Basic.lean:649) is exactly the image of `x` under a definable function `f`, characterized by `ZFSet.mem_image`.

annotations.label: Axiom schema of replacement (formal statement)

anchor.snippet: The axiom schema of replacement states that if

annotations.note: `ZFSet.mem_image` together with the existence of `ZFSet.image` realises the replacement schema: for any `Definable₁ f` and `x`, the set `{f z | z ∈ x}` exists.

annotations.label: Replacement implied by limitation of size

anchor.snippet: It is implied by the stronger axiom of limitation of size

annotations.note: Grepping `Mathlib` for `limitation of size`/`LimitationOfSize` returns no results; this axiom is not formalized.

annotations.label: Existence of ω·2 requires replacement

anchor.snippet: proving the existence of any limit ordinal greater than

annotations.note: The ordinal ω·2 exists in Mathlib via `Ordinal.omega0 * 2` (e.g. `Ordinal.mul_two`), but the metamathematical claim that its existence requires replacement is not formalized.

annotations.label: Construction of the first uncountable ordinal

anchor.snippet: the first uncountable ordinal

annotations.note: `ω₁ = ω_ 1 = Ordinal.omega 1` is defined in `Mathlib/SetTheory/Cardinal/Aleph.lean` as the first uncountable ordinal.

annotations.label: Hartogs number generalization

anchor.snippet: This is a special case of the result of Hartogs number

annotations.note: Greps for `Hartogs`/`hartogs` in `Mathlib/SetTheory` return no matches; the Hartogs number construction is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ordinal assignment to well-ordered sets requires replacement

anchor.snippet: the existence of an assignment of an ordinal to every well-ordered set requires replacement

annotations.note: `Ordinal.type` assigns an ordinal to every well-ordered type, but the metatheorem that this assignment requires the replacement axiom is not formalized.

annotations.label: Von Neumann cardinal assignment requires replacement

anchor.snippet: the von Neumann cardinal assignment

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Cardinal.mk` as a quotient-based cardinal assignment, but the meta-statement that the von Neumann assignment requires replacement is not formalized.

annotations.label: High-rank tuple sets unprovable without replacement

anchor.snippet: has too high of a rank for its existence to be provable from set theory

annotations.note: This is a metamathematical independence claim about Z without replacement; no such statement appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Borel determinacy theorem (Martin)

anchor.snippet: Borel determinacy theorem

annotations.note: Greps for `BorelDeterminacy`/`borel_determinacy` yield no relevant Mathlib declarations.

annotations.label: ZF proves consistency of Z

anchor.snippet: ZF (which includes replacement) proves the consistency of Z

annotations.note: Mathlib does not contain a formal consistency statement Con(Z) provable in ZF; no `Consistency` declarations were found in `Mathlib/SetTheory`.

annotations.label: Lévy's parameter-free replacement equivalence

anchor.snippet: a version of replacement with parameters removed

annotations.note: Greps for `Lévy`/`Levy`/`reflection` in `Mathlib/SetTheory` return no matches; no parameter-free replacement equivalence is formalized.

annotations.label: Axiom schema of collection

anchor.snippet: The axiom schema of collection is closely related to and frequently confused with the axiom schema of replacement

annotations.note: Searching `Mathlib/SetTheory/ZFC` finds only `EmptyCollection`/`powerset` collections; no axiom schema of collection.

annotations.label: Collection equivalent to replacement over ZF

anchor.snippet: Over the remainder of the ZF axioms, it is equivalent to the axiom schema of replacement

annotations.note: Since neither schema is formally stated in Mathlib, the equivalence between them is not formalized either.

annotations.label: Collection stronger than replacement without power set

anchor.snippet: The axiom of collection is stronger than replacement in the absence of the power set axiom

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formal treatment of subsystems of ZF without powerset comparing collection and replacement.

annotations.label: Axiom schema of boundedness

anchor.snippet: The resulting axiom schema is also called the axiom schema of boundedness

annotations.note: No `Boundedness` axiom schema is defined in `Mathlib/SetTheory/ZFC`.

annotations.label: Separation follows from replacement and empty set

anchor.snippet: is implied by the axiom schema of replacement and the axiom of empty set

annotations.note: `ZFSet.sep` (Mathlib/SetTheory/ZFC/Basic.lean:415) provides separation, and `ZFSet.image` provides replacement, but the metatheorem deriving one from the other is not formalized.

annotations.label: ZFC axiomatizable by a single infinite schema

anchor.snippet: it is possible to axiomatize ZFC with a single infinite axiom schema

annotations.note: This is a metalogical statement about axiomatizations of ZFC; nothing of the kind is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lévy's reflection principle equivalent to replacement

anchor.snippet: Lévy's reflection principle for ZFC is equivalent to the axiom schema of replacement

annotations.note: No reflection principle or Lévy-style equivalence with replacement is found in Mathlib.
